scene.name: The Pallid Hart — Crossroads Tavern
scene.description: A small tavern at a crossroads deep in the Orava mountains. The locals call it a cursed junction — where the road from the north meets the old pilgrim trail from the west. The old legends say the devil himself walks crossroads at night, offering bargains to desperate travelers. Nobody travels these forests after dark. Only the most desperate or the most foolish venture onto these roads at night — the wolves are bold, the paths treacherous, and things far worse than wolves have been seen moving between the trees. Yet here you all are. Some of you have been sitting in this cramped, firelit room for an hour or more, others arrived only minutes ago. You do not know each other. The blizzard outside has sealed you in together.
scene.arrivalNotes: All PCs start in the tavern. They have been here varying lengths of time. They do not know each other — use anonymous token mode (tokens show as 'Traveler'). Each player can see the others but knows nothing about them beyond what they observe. Describe what each PC sees: the other strangers in the room, the patrons, the nervous innkeeper, the hooded figure in the corner.

scenes.name: The Pallid Hart — Crossroads Tavern
scenes.description: A small tavern at a crossroads in the Orava mountains. The room is cramped, low-ceilinged, dark wood. A fire crackles in a stone hearth. A bar runs along the far wall where a nervous innkeeper polishes the same glass endlessly. A hooded figure sits motionless in the far corner. The cellar door is closed, a cloth draped over its handle. Outside, a blizzard seals the doors shut.
scenes.readAloud: You have each been sitting in this small tavern for a while now — some of you for only a few minutes, others for an hour or more. You have watched people come in from the storm, shaking snow from their cloaks, stamping feeling back into frozen boots. The Pallid Hart sits where two roads cross in the mountains — the north road from the villages and the old pilgrim trail from the west. The locals say crossroads are cursed places. Where the devil walks at midnight, offering bargains to the desperate. Nobody travels these forests at night. The wolves are bold, the paths treacherous, and the old-timers whisper of things far worse than wolves moving between the trees after dark. Yet here you all are — strangers driven together by a blizzard that fell like a hammer. The common room is cramped, low-ceilinged, dark wood. A fire crackles in the stone hearth, casting long shadows. Behind the bar, a thin, dark-haired woman grips a glass she has been polishing far too long. Her eyes dart to every sound. In the far corner, almost lost in shadow, a hooded figure sits motionless at a table. They have not moved since you arrived. They have not spoken. You do not know each other's names.

scenes.name: Mountain Road — Blizzard (Flashback)
scenes.description: The mountain road approach. Can be used for flashback sequences — what each PC saw on the road before reaching the inn.
scenes.readAloud: The wind cuts through your cloak like a knife made of ice. Snow hammers your face until you can barely see ten feet ahead. The mountain road is a guess now — a memory of packed earth beneath the drifts. The trees press close on either side, their branches creaking and groaning. And then — ahead, barely visible through the white curtain — a warm orange glow. A building. An inn.

initial.description: Heavy blizzard — snow hammers the shutters, wind screams

phases.description: The blizzard intensifies. The shutters rattle. Snow piles against the door.

data.text: The storm outside is getting worse. Describe the wind howling louder, snow pressing against the windows.

phases.description: The storm peaks. Thunder rolls through the mountains. The building groans.

data.text: Thunder cracks — not normal thunder. It sounds like something enormous moving through the mountains. The whole inn shudders.

phases.description: The storm begins to fade. The wind drops. An eerie silence grows.

data.text: The storm is dying down. The silence is almost worse than the wind. Dawn is coming.

phases.description: The storm has passed. Pale dawn light creeps through the shutters. Snow lies deep and still.

data.text: Dawn breaks. The blizzard is over. Thin light pushes through every crack. The world outside is white and silent.

data.description: The last light dies. Full darkness outside.
The fire burns lower. The room grows colder. Shadows stretch.
data.text: The fire is burning low — describe the room growing darker and colder. Marta hasn't added wood.

data.description: The full moon rises behind the storm clouds. Tomas feels it.
Tomas is visibly sweating despite the cold. His hands are shaking.
data.text: Tomas is sweating heavily now. His hands tremble. If any player is watching him, they notice. Perception DC 10 to see the scratches on his forearms.

data.description: Scratching sounds from beneath the floor. The cellar.
data.readAloud: A sound. From below. Scritch. Scritch-scritch. Like nails on wood. Everyone hears it. Marta freezes.

checks.success: It's coming from directly under the bar — the cellar.

data.description: Vladislav studies the party from the shadows
data.text: The hooded stranger shifts slightly. For the first time, you catch the glint of eyes beneath the hood — watching. Not the room. Watching one specific player. The one who seems most isolated or frightened. Which player is that right now?

data.description: Midnight approaches. Tomas is desperate. Vladislav is hungry.
data.text: It's nearly midnight. Tomas MUST get to the cellar or he transforms in front of everyone. Vladislav is growing bold — he's been patient long enough. Both will act within the hour.

data.description: Midnight. Tomas can no longer resist. He transforms.
Dawn breaks. The blizzard subsides. Vladislav must retreat or die.

data.text: Katya stops playing mid-song. She stares at the brass plate behind the bar. She's seen the stranger's missing reflection. She catches a player's eye and subtly shakes her head — she won't say it aloud. But she KNOWS.

The fire gutters and dies completely. The room plunges into near-darkness — only a single candle on Aldric's table remains. The temperature drops sharply. This is Vladislav's doing — he's drawing the heat from the room. Set atmosphere to dread_rising if not already there.

Old Gregor suddenly clutches his chest and collapses. Is it a heart attack? Or did something happen to him when no one was looking? Medicine DC 12 reveals two small puncture wounds on his neck. Vladislav fed on him when the room was distracted.

Tomas stands abruptly. He can't wait any longer — the moon is pulling at him. He walks toward the cellar door with grim determination. Vladislav stiffens. Both monsters are now converging on the same location. What do the players do?

Brother Aldric begins praying loudly. His holy symbol glows faintly — genuine faith responding to the evil in the room. Vladislav REACTS: he turns his head toward Aldric for the first time, and his lips pull back in something between a snarl and a smile. The players see fangs. BRIEFLY. Then the hood falls forward again.

cues: The wind shifts direction suddenly. For a moment you hear something in it — was that a voice?
A branch cracks somewhere in the darkness to your left. Something heavy.
Your boot catches on something in the snow. A dead bird. Frozen solid. Its eyes are open.
The trees thin for a moment and you see the sky — bruised purple, the last light bleeding away. You need shelter. Now.
You smell iron. Blood. It's faint but unmistakable. The snow ahead has a dark stain that the fresh fall hasn't quite covered.
The fire pops and everyone flinches. Marta drops the glass she was holding. It doesn't break but the sound is too loud in the silence.
A gust of wind finds its way through the shutters. The candles on the bar gutter and almost die. For a moment, the stranger's face is visible — pale, angular, wrong somehow.
Something creaks upstairs. Footsteps? But no one has gone up.
Marta glances at the cellar door again. She does this every few minutes. She doesn't seem to realize she's doing it.
The wind screams and the front door rattles in its frame. For a moment it sounds like something is trying the handle from outside.
Old Gregor coughs — a wet, rattling sound. He looks worse than when the evening started. Paler.
Katya the minstrel stops humming mid-note. She stares at a spot on the wall for a long moment, then shakes her head and looks away.
The shadows in the corner where the stranger sits seem deeper than they should be. The firelight doesn't reach there. It should.
A draft from somewhere carries a smell — earth, damp stone, something sweet and rotting. From below.
Tomas drops his drink. His hands are shaking badly now. He doesn't pick it up.

secrets.description: The hooded stranger is Vladislav Dragan, a vampire. He is responsible for the disappearances.
discoveryMethods: Find his coffin in the cellar
secrets.revealConsequences: Vladislav drops pretense. He is either confronted directly or begins openly hunting. Atmosphere shifts to terror_peak.

secrets.description: Tomas Birkov is a werewolf. He transforms at the full moon, which is tonight.
discoveryMethods: Catch him trying to lock himself away
See the full moon trigger him
secrets.revealConsequences: If revealed early, Tomas begs for help. If transformation happens publicly, panic — possible combat. He is not evil, just cursed.

secrets.description: Marta's husband Piotr was turned into a vampire spawn by Vladislav. He is in the cellar.
discoveryMethods: Enter the cellar and find him
Marta breaks down and reveals she heard his voice
secrets.revealConsequences: Marta is devastated. The spawn may attack. This is the emotional core of the session.

secrets.description: The cellar contains Vladislav's coffin, Piotr as a vampire spawn, and the drained bodies of two missing travelers.
discoveryMethods: Open the cellar door and go down
secrets.revealConsequences: Everything becomes clear. The full horror of what has been happening. Multiple secrets may reveal at once.

secrets.description: Both Tomas (to lock himself in before transformation) and Vladislav (to protect his coffin) want access to the cellar. They are in silent competition.
discoveryMethods: Watch both NPCs' behavior toward the cellar
Ask Tomas why he keeps looking at the cellar door
secrets.revealConsequences: Players realize the cellar is the key to everything. Increases tension dramatically.

secrets.description: The Frascht family are legendary monster hunters — a Van Helsing bloodline known and feared across the supernatural world for generations. Barry's parents were active hunters who died fighting Vladislav (or his network). Barry has no memory of this — he was too young. The genie patron knows the full truth but is forbidden from revealing it. Barry's 'paranoid' gear (stakes, holy water, mirror, manacles) is instinct — the hunter blood calling. Vladislav recognizes the Frascht name immediately and is both fascinated and wary. The last Frascht, untrained, sitting in his lair.
discoveryMethods: Vladislav lets it slip in dialogue
Barry finds a family journal or symbol
Tomas or another monster recognizes the name
Katya knows a ballad about the Frascht hunters
The genie breaks its silence in a moment of desperation
secrets.revealConsequences: Barry's entire identity shifts. The 'imaginary friend' is real. His parents were heroes, not ordinary people. Vladislav killed them. Barry must choose: embrace the family legacy or reject it. This is a campaign-spanning revelation — do NOT reveal it all at once. Plant seeds in Session 0, pay it off over the arc.
secrets.aiInstructions: CRITICAL: This is the biggest player-specific secret in the campaign. Vladislav KNOWS the Frascht name. When he hears it, he goes still — just for a moment. He studies Barry differently from the other players. He is careful, respectful, almost nostalgic. He does NOT attack Barry directly unless forced. He wants to understand how much Barry knows. Drop TINY hints in Session 0 only: a reaction to the name, a comment about 'old families', Vladislav watching Barry's hands when he handles the stakes. Do NOT reveal the full truth yet. The genie should be unusually agitated — it knows where they are and what Vladislav is. If Barry talks to his 'imaginary friend', the genie is terrified but cannot explain why.

clues.description: A deer carcass found drained of blood. Old Gregor saw it on the road. Or a PC noticed it through the window during a lull in the storm.
clues.readAloud: Old Gregor leans forward, his voice dropping. 'On the road. Coming here tonight. I found a deer. Dead. Not killed by wolves — I've seen wolf kills my whole life. This was... empty. Drained. Like a wineskin someone squeezed dry. Not a drop of blood in the body. Not a drop on the snow. And the wounds — two holes. Small. Right over the throat.' He takes a long drink. 'My grandmother would have known what did that. She had a word for it. Strigoi.'

clues.description: Marta's husband Piotr disappeared three weeks ago. She believes he's dead.
clues.readAloud: Marta's eyes go hollow when you ask. 'Piotr. My husband.' Her voice is barely a whisper. 'Three weeks. He went down to the cellar to get more wine and he... he didn't come back. I looked. I looked everywhere. The cellar was empty. He was just... gone.'

clues.description: Scratching and scraping sounds from the cellar. Regular, rhythmic, like something pacing.
clues.readAloud: There. Again. From below your feet — a scratching sound. Rhythmic. Patient. Like something down there is pacing. Back and forth. Back and forth.

clues.description: Deep scratches on Tomas's forearms, self-inflicted during his last transformation.
clues.readAloud: As Tomas reaches for his drink, his sleeve rides up. Deep gouges run along his forearm — not cuts, but scratches. Like claws. And they're not old. The skin around them is still angry red.

clues.description: Tomas keeps glancing at the cellar door with increasing desperation.
clues.readAloud: You notice Tomas staring at the cellar door. Not casually — with a desperate, haunted intensity. His jaw is clenched. His knuckles are white around his mug.

clues.description: The stranger also watches the cellar door, but with cold possessiveness.
clues.readAloud: The stranger's head turns — slowly, deliberately — toward the cellar door. Not with curiosity. With ownership. Like someone checking a lock on their own front door.

clues.description: The stranger casts no reflection in the bar's polished copper or any reflective surface.
clues.readAloud: The copper pans behind the bar are polished enough to catch reflections. You can see Marta. You can see yourself. But the corner where the stranger sits — it's empty in the copper. As if no one is there at all.

clues.description: The stranger always positions himself as far from the fire as possible and flinches from flame.
clues.readAloud: When Marta passes with a candle, the stranger leans away — sharply, instinctively, like someone avoiding a hot brand. His chair scrapes. He catches himself and goes still again.

clues.description: An ornate coffin in the cellar, soil from a distant land packed inside.
clues.readAloud: The cellar is cold. Not winter cold — something else. Something wrong. And there, against the far wall, draped in shadow — a coffin. Dark wood, brass fittings, packed with black soil. It doesn't belong here. It belongs in a crypt.

clues.description: Two shriveled corpses in the cellar — the missing travelers.
clues.readAloud: Behind the wine barrels. Two shapes. Two people. What's left of two people. They're dried husks — like the deer on the road. Drained. Discarded.

clues.description: Piotr Kowalski, now a vampire spawn, chained in the cellar shadows.
clues.readAloud: A rattling. Chains. Something moves in the darkest corner. A man — or what was a man. He's chained to the wall, emaciated, but his eyes burn red in the darkness. His mouth opens and you see fangs. 'Help... me...' The voice is a rasp. 'He... made me... this...'

clues.description: Vladislav reacts when he hears the name Frascht — a moment of stillness, recognition, danger.
clues.triggerCondition: Barry introduces himself by surname or another player uses it
clues.readAloud: The hooded stranger goes still. Not the casual stillness of someone listening — a predator's stillness. Like a snake that has just scented something unexpected. It lasts only a moment. Then the hood turns, slowly, toward Barry. You can't see the face. But you can feel the attention.

clues.description: Barry's gear marks him as a monster hunter to anyone who knows what to look for.
clues.readAloud: You notice the newcomer's pack. Wooden stakes — nine of them. A flask of holy water. A steel mirror. Manacles. That's not a traveler's kit. That's a hunter's kit. Whoever this person is, they came prepared for something specific.

clues.description: Barry's 'imaginary friend' (genie patron) is unusually agitated inside the inn. If Barry tries to talk to it, it's terrified but can't say why.
clues.readAloud: Your imaginary friend hasn't spoken since you entered the inn. That's unusual — normally you can't shut them up. When you reach out, there's... static. Fear. A feeling like a hand gripping your wrist, pulling you toward the door. Then one word, barely a whisper: 'Leave.'

discoveryChains.description: The chain of evidence leading to the vampire reveal
The trail from Marta's grief to Piotr's fate

branches.description: Players find the cellar before Vladislav reveals himself — they have evidence and initiative

data.text: Players found the evidence first. Vladislav knows they know — he must act. He can try to flee, fight, or negotiate. His disposition determines which.

branches.description: Vladislav strikes before players find the cellar — they are blindsided

data.text: Vladislav acts first. The players don't know what he is yet. This is a horror reveal, not a confrontation. Play it for maximum dread.

branches.description: Tomas transforms in the common room — chaos erupts, two monsters in the inn

data.text: Tomas transforms. Two monsters in one inn. This is the worst-case scenario and the most dramatic. Vladislav may use the chaos to feed or escape.

futureHooks.description: Marta's husband Piotr is a vampire spawn in the cellar — if players save or destroy him, Marta becomes an ally or enemy in future sessions
futureHooks.payoffCondition: Players discover Piotr's fate and make a choice
futureHooks.notes: If players promise to help Piotr, Marta provides safe haven at The Pallid Hart for future sessions

futureHooks.description: If Vladislav escapes the inn alive, he becomes a recurring antagonist across the three-castle arc
futureHooks.notes: Vladislav flees toward Houska (Castle 2). Players may encounter him again with new spawn.

futureHooks.description: Tomas Birkov's werewolf curse — if players learn of it and let him live, he may seek their help curing it
futureHooks.payoffCondition: Players interact with Tomas's lycanthropy without killing him
futureHooks.notes: Tomas knows mountain routes to Houska. Could become guide or enemy.

futureHooks.description: The cellar contains evidence of a larger vampire network — letters referencing Houska Castle and 'the gathering'
futureHooks.notes: This plants the seed for the Houska arc (sessions 2-4)

factions.name: Mountain Villagers
factions.description: The scattered communities of the Orava mountain region. Superstitious, fearful, desperate for help.

factions.name: The Church of Light
factions.description: Local clergy and inquisitors. Suspicious of outsiders but aligned against undead. Brother Aldric is their representative.

factions.name: Vladislav's Brood
factions.description: The vampire lord's growing network of spawn, thralls, and unwitting servants.

backstories._note: Fill in each PC's reason for being at the crossroads once character sheets are imported. Use appearance data from DDB for physical descriptions shown to other players.

jerome.reasonAtCrossroads: TODO — fill in once character background is finalized
jerome.whatOthersSee: TODO — pull from character appearance data (age 40, 5'11", messy brown hair, gray/hazel eyes, weathered skin, carries wooden stakes and holy water)

lore.title: The Pallid Hart
lore.content: A small inn at a crossroads deep in the Orava mountains — where the north road from the villages meets the old pilgrim trail from the west. Run by Marta Kowalski and her husband Piotr (now missing). Named for a ghostly white stag said to appear at the crossroads before death comes to the mountains. The cellar is older than the inn — locals say it was built over the foundations of a monastery that was abandoned under mysterious circumstances. The crossroads itself has a dark reputation: the old legends say the devil walks crossroads at midnight, offering bargains to desperate travelers. Locals leave offerings of bread and salt at the junction and avoid it after dark.

lore.title: Crossroads — The Devil's Junction
lore.content: In Central European folklore, crossroads are liminal places — thresholds between worlds where the boundaries thin. The devil is said to appear at crossroads at midnight to offer bargains: power, wealth, love, or knowledge, always at a terrible price. Travelers who linger at crossroads after dark risk encountering wandering spirits, demons, or the restless dead. In 1274, these beliefs are deeply held — peasants leave offerings of bread and salt, hang iron nails above the junction, and never travel the crossing after sunset. The Pallid Hart was built at such a crossroads, which locals see as either brave or foolish. The fact that travelers keep disappearing from this particular crossroads has only strengthened the superstition.

lore.title: Nobody Travels at Night
lore.content: In 1274, traveling the mountain forests of the Orava region after dark is considered suicidal. The roads are barely more than packed-earth trails, unlit, with no milestones or markers. Wolf packs are bold and coordinated — they take livestock and have been known to attack travelers. Bears emerge from hibernation unpredictably. But the real fear is older and deeper: the strigoi. The walking dead. Things that drink blood and wear human faces. Every village has stories. Every family has lost someone on these roads who was never found. Only merchants on tight schedules, pilgrims with more faith than sense, soldiers under orders, and locals who know the trails like their own hands will travel these mountains — and even they stop at sunset. Anyone still on the road after dark has either made a terrible mistake or is running from something worse than what waits in the dark.

lore.title: Vladislav Dragan — The Hooded Stranger
lore.content: A vampire lord posing as a weary traveler. He arrived at The Pallid Hart two days ago. His true purpose: to feed on isolated travelers and expand his brood. He created Piotr as a spawn and keeps him in the cellar. Vladislav is connected to a larger vampire network centered on Houska Castle in Bohemia.

lore.title: Tomas Birkov — The Trapper
lore.content: A werewolf disguised as a mountain trapper. Tomas was bitten three months ago and is losing control. He came to the inn seeking the cellar — he heard rumors of old chains in the monastery foundations where he could restrain himself during the full moon. He does not know about Vladislav.

lore.title: The Orava Mountains
lore.content: A remote, heavily forested mountain range in the northern Slovak region of the Kingdom of Hungary. In 1274, these mountains are sparsely populated, with scattered villages connected by dangerous mountain roads. Wolves, bears, and worse things prowl the forests. The region has a dark reputation — travelers disappear, livestock is found drained of blood, and the locals whisper about things that walk at night.

lore.title: The Three Castles
lore.content: Three ancient castles form the backbone of the vampire conspiracy: Orava Castle in the Slovak mountains (the starting region), Houska Castle in Bohemia (said to be built over a gateway to Hell), and Čachtice Castle in western Hungary (seat of a noble family with dark secrets). The Dark Pilgrimage follows this path.

downtimeEvents.description: Wolf packs have been moving closer to the mountain road, driven by something in the deep forest

data.text: Between sessions: wolves closing in on the road. Travelers report larger packs than normal.

downtimeEvents.description: Another traveler has gone missing on the mountain road — the third this month

data.text: Between sessions: another disappearance reported. Villagers are talking about closing the road.

marta.name: Marta Kowalski
knowledge: Her husband Piotr disappeared three weeks ago
Two other travelers vanished from the inn recently
Scratching sounds from the cellar at night
A dead animal drained of blood found behind the inn
The hooded stranger arrived two days ago and barely speaks

goals.goal: Keep everyone calm and survive until morning
Warn guests if danger becomes undeniable
actions: Reveals everything she knows about the cellar sounds and missing husband

dialogueHints.husband: Deflects at first, tears up, eventually admits he vanished going to the cellar
dialogueHints.cellar: Visibly frightened. 'Don't go down there. Please. Just... don't.'
dialogueHints.stranger: 'He came two days ago. Paid in coin. Doesn't eat. Doesn't drink. Just... sits.'
dialogueHints.sounds: 'You hear them too? I thought I was going mad. Every night. From below.'
dialogueHints.blood: 'The goat behind the inn. Drained dry. Like something sucked the life from it.'
dialogueHints.when_vampire_revealed: Collapses. 'I KNEW. I knew what he was. I could feel it. But I couldn't— I didn't know how to— Oh God, Piotr. What did he do to Piotr?'
dialogueHints.when_tomas_transforms: Screams and dives behind the bar. 'TWO of them?! There are TWO?!'
dialogueHints.when_confronted_about_silence: 'What was I supposed to do?! He's a MONSTER. You think I could fight that? I'm an innkeeper! I kept quiet because keeping quiet kept me alive!'
dialogueHints.when_piotr_found: Wailing. 'That's not him. That's not my Piotr. Please... please help him. Or end it. I can't watch this anymore.'

tomas.name: Tomas Birkov
tomas.role: Nervous trapper sitting near the door
knowledge: He is a werewolf, cursed two years ago
The full moon rises tonight — he cannot control the transformation
He came to the inn hoping to lock himself in the cellar before moonrise
He can smell something unnatural in the inn but doesn't know it's a vampire
He knows these mountain woods intimately — every trail, cave, and den
The scratches on his arms are self-inflicted from his last transformation

goals.goal: Get into the cellar and lock himself in before the moon rises
actions: Ask Marta about the cellar casually
Offer to fetch wine as excuse

data.text: Tomas has reached the cellar. If Vladislav's coffin is there, they will encounter each other.

data.description: Tomas transforms in the common room

goals.goal: Keep his lycanthropy secret from everyone

dialogueHints.scratches: Pulls sleeve down quickly. 'Wolf. On the road. It's nothing.' (lie)
dialogueHints.cellar: 'Is there a cellar? I thought I might... fetch some wine. Help out.' (desperate)
dialogueHints.nervousness: 'The storm. Just the storm. I don't like being trapped.' (half-truth)
dialogueHints.stranger: 'Something about him. Something wrong. I can... I just don't trust him.' (he can smell the undead)
dialogueHints.woods: Opens up here — knows every trail, every cave. This is genuine and helpful.
dialogueHints.moonrise: Goes pale. Changes subject immediately. If pressed: 'I need air. I need to— I need to go somewhere.'
dialogueHints.when_exposed: Desperate, broken. 'I didn't ASK for this! I was bitten three months ago. I came here to lock myself in the cellar before the moon. I'm trying to PROTECT you!'
dialogueHints.when_transforming: Screaming, bones cracking. 'RUN! Get away from me! I can't— I can't stop it— PLEASE—' Then the screaming stops and the growling starts.
dialogueHints.when_vampire_revealed: Vindicated, angry. 'I TOLD you something was wrong with him! I could SMELL it. The dead. He reeks of the dead.'
dialogueHints.when_offered_help: Tears. Genuine hope for the first time. 'You would... help me? No one has offered to help. Everyone runs. Or tries to kill me. I know a path to Houska — there are people there who study curses. I could guide you.'

revealTriggers: The full moon becomes visible through a broken shutter or opened door
He is badly wounded and the beast takes over
Someone examines his wounds closely (DC 14 Medicine/Nature)
Midnight approaches and he becomes desperate to reach the cellar

hooded-stranger.name: The Stranger
knowledge: He is the vampire responsible for the disappearances
He killed the missing travelers
He has a coffin hidden in the cellar
He senses which players are most vulnerable
Dawn will force him to retreat

goals.goal: Prevent anyone from entering the cellar and discovering his coffin
actions: Subtly redirect anyone who approaches it

goals.goal: Feed on an isolated guest before dawn
actions: Identify the weakest or most isolated guest

data.text: Vladislav has fed. A patron or PC has been attacked. If not discovered, he will be emboldened.

goals.goal: Eliminate anyone who discovers his true nature
actions: Attack or attempt to flee to cellar

dialogueHints.name: '...You may call me Vladislav.' Pause. As if he had to remember his own name.
dialogueHints.purpose: 'Traveling. Like you.' His voice is cultured. Old. Wrong for these mountains.
dialogueHints.food: 'I have already eaten.' He has not touched the plate Marta set before him.
dialogueHints.cellar: Goes very still. 'I wouldn't go down there. Old buildings. Unstable floors.' (threatening undertone)
dialogueHints.weather: 'The storm will pass. They always do. The question is what survives the night.'
dialogueHints.missing_people: 'People go missing in the mountains all the time. The cold. The wolves.' A faint smile.
dialogueHints.direct_accusation: Stands slowly. The shadows seem to deepen around him. 'You should be very careful what you accuse a stranger of.'
dialogueHints.when_exposed: Drops all pretense. 'How long has it been since you've seen something truly ancient? I have walked these mountains since before your great-grandparents were born. You are meat. Clever meat, but meat nonetheless.'
dialogueHints.when_fighting: 'I offered you a peaceful death. You chose the other kind.'
dialogueHints.when_wounded: Shock — then fury. 'You DARE? Do you know what I am? I am the lord of this mountain. I am the death that walks the road. And you have just made your last mistake.'
dialogueHints.when_fleeing: 'This is not over. I remember faces. I remember names. I remember the taste of fear. And yours...' (dissolves into mist) '...is exquisite.'
dialogueHints.to_tomas: 'Ah. The wolf. I can smell the beast in you. We are not so different, you and I. Both cursed. Both hungry. Perhaps we can help each other.' (manipulation)
dialogueHints.to_piotr: 'Rise.' Simple command. Piotr obeys instantly, a puppet on invisible strings.

revealTriggers: Someone enters the cellar and finds his coffin
Holy symbol brandished with genuine faith
Dawn approaches and he becomes desperate

patron-farmer.name: Old Gregor
knowledge: Has lived in these mountains his whole life
Knows the old legends — vampires, werewolves, the white stag
His livestock have been dying — drained of blood
He came to the inn to warn Marta but lost his nerve

dialogueHints.legends: 'My grandmother told me stories. About things in the mountains that drink blood. I thought they were stories.' (long pause) 'They weren't.'
dialogueHints.livestock: 'Three goats. A cow. Found them at dawn, white as snow. Not a drop of blood in them. Not a drop on the ground.'
dialogueHints.stranger: 'Don't look at him. Don't talk to him. Don't go near him. I've seen eyes like that before. On a dead man.'
dialogueHints.stag: 'The pallid hart. The white stag. My grandmother said it appears before death comes to these mountains. I saw it on the road tonight.'
dialogueHints.hope: Shakes his head. 'We're not leaving this inn alive. I've known it since I walked in.'

patron-merchant.name: Henryk the Merchant
knowledge: Travels this road regularly — knows it's dangerous
Has heard rumors of disappearances but dismissed them
Carries valuable goods and is afraid of thieves
Noticed the stranger doesn't eat or drink

dialogueHints.road: 'I've traveled this road twenty times. Never seen a storm this bad. Never felt this... wrong.'
dialogueHints.stranger: 'Man hasn't touched his food. Hasn't moved. I've been watching — he doesn't even blink. That's not natural.'
dialogueHints.business: Perks up briefly about trade, then catches himself. 'None of that matters if we don't make it through the night.'
dialogueHints.disappearances: 'People go missing on mountain roads. It happens. But three in one month? From the same inn?' (whispers) 'That doesn't happen.'
dialogueHints.danger: 'I'll pay double for an escort out of here at first light. Triple. Name your price.'

patron-pilgrim.name: Brother Aldric
knowledge: Traveling to a monastery in the south
His faith is genuine — he can sense evil nearby
He has blessed water in his pack (holy water)
He knows the rites of turning undead but has never performed them

dialogueHints.prayer: 'I have been praying since I entered this place. The words... they feel important here. As if something is listening.'
dialogueHints.evil: 'There is evil in this room. I can feel it. My training taught me to recognize the presence of the unholy. It is here. Close.'
dialogueHints.stranger: 'I cannot look at him. My hand goes to my symbol every time I try. Something in my faith recoils from what sits in that corner.'
dialogueHints.holy_water: If asked directly: 'I carry blessed water. For the journey. I... yes. You may have it. I think you will need it more than I.'
dialogueHints.courage: 'I am a man of faith, not a man of war. But if this is God's test, I will not fail it. Tell me what to do.'

patron-minstrel.name: Katya Voss
knowledge: Travels widely and collects stories
Has sharp eyes — notices details others miss
Heard of the Pallid Hart's dark reputation before arriving
She noticed Tomas's scars and the stranger's lack of reflection

dialogueHints.stories: 'I collect stories. That's what minstrels do. And this inn? This inn is going to be one hell of a story. If we survive to tell it.'
dialogueHints.crossroads_tale: When the room feels quiet or needs energy, Katya begins telling a tale: 'Shall I earn my supper? I know a story about this very crossroads. They say a monastery once stood here — the Brothers of the Pallid Light. They built their chapel over the crossroads to sanctify it, because the old ones said the devil walked this junction at midnight. The brothers prayed and rang their bells and for a hundred years, nothing stirred. Then one winter — a winter like this one — the bells stopped. Travelers found the monastery empty. Every brother gone. No blood, no struggle, no sign of flight. Just empty cells and a chapel door hanging open. The only thing left was a white stag, standing in the crossroads at dawn, staring at the empty building with human eyes.' She pauses. 'The inn was built over the foundations. The cellar is the old monastery cellar.' She glances at the cellar door. 'I wonder if the brothers ever came back up.'
dialogueHints.observation: 'The trapper is hiding something under his sleeves. The stranger has no reflection — I saw it in the brass plate. And the innkeeper is terrified of her own cellar.'
dialogueHints.music: 'I tried to play earlier. The acoustics in here are wrong. The sound... echoes differently. As if the room is bigger than it should be. Or as if something underground is resonating.'
dialogueHints.stranger: 'He's been watching us. All of us. But especially the ones who go near the cellar door. I've been watching him watch us.'
dialogueHints.help: 'I'm not a fighter. But I'm clever, I'm fast, and I have very good ears. Tell me what you need.'
dialogueHints.frascht_ballad: If she hears the name Frascht, her eyes widen. 'Frascht? THE Frascht family? There's a ballad about them — the Hunters of the Eastern Marches. My grandmother taught it to me. They say the Fraschts could smell a strigoi from a mile away. That they carried silver and holy water like other men carry bread and wine.' She looks at Barry with new interest. 'Are you one of THOSE Fraschts?'

performances._note: Katya performs throughout the early evening, providing atmospheric background and dropping lore clues in songs and tales. The AI should use these to generate ambient performance text. As the mood darkens, her songs become darker. She stops performing entirely when the atmosphere shifts to dread_rising.

tavern_warm.title: The Mountain Road
tavern_warm.content: Katya strums a gentle melody and sings: 'O the mountain road is long and cold, the mountain road is steep / Where travelers walk by morning light but dare not walk in sleep / For what moves between the pines at night no living man should meet / And the only sound you hear at dawn is the crunching of the feet.' She trails off with a smile. 'Just a folk song. Nothing to worry about.'

tavern_warm.title: The Devil's Crossroads
tavern_warm.content: Katya pauses her playing. 'Do you know why crossroads are cursed? They say the devil cannot walk a straight road — he can only appear where paths divide. Where a traveler must choose. That's the bargain, you see. The choice itself is the trap. You think you're choosing left or right, but you're really choosing what you're willing to lose.' She glances at the crossroads visible through the frosted window. 'This inn was built on one of the oldest crossroads in the mountains. The locals won't come here after dark.'

tavern_warm.title: The White Stag
tavern_warm.content: A haunting, minor-key melody: 'The pallid hart walks at the edge of dawn / With eyes like winter moons / It stands upon the crossroads where / The old monks sang their tunes / And if you see it watching you / With gaze so cold and white / Then say your prayers and bar the door / For death comes with the night.' The fire pops. Katya stops. 'That one always gives me shivers in a place like this.'

tavern_tense.title: The Strigoi
tavern_tense.content: Katya's voice drops lower. 'My grandmother — she was from these mountains — she told me about the strigoi. The undead who drink blood. She said they look like us. Walk like us. Talk like us. But they cannot eat. Cannot drink. And they cast no shadow, no reflection. She said the only way to know one is to watch what they DON'T do.' A long pause. She does not look at the stranger's corner.

tavern_tense.title: The Lost Monastery
tavern_tense.content: Katya sets down her lute. 'I asked about this inn in the last village. The old woman at the well crossed herself and told me the story. A monastery stood here once — the Brothers of the Pallid Light. They built their chapel over the crossroads to sanctify it. For a hundred years, they kept vigil, praying and ringing their bells through every night. Then one winter, the bells stopped. The monastery was empty. Every brother gone. No blood, no sign of struggle. Just empty cells and a chapel door hanging open.' She glances toward the cellar. 'This inn was built over the foundations. That cellar down there? That's the old monastery cellar.'

tavern_tense.title: The Bargain
tavern_tense.content: Barely above a whisper, she sings: 'At the crossroads came a stranger tall / With a smile like frozen bone / He said I'll grant you anything / If you will walk alone / Through the forest dark and terrible / Where the dead things make their bed / And bring me back the living heart / Of someone that you love instead.' She stops abruptly. 'Sorry. That one's too dark for a night like this.'

beats.name: Strangers at the Crossroads
beats.readAloud:
You have each been sitting in this small tavern for a while now — some of you for only a few minutes, others for an hour or more. You have watched people come in from the storm, shaking snow from their cloaks, stamping feeling back into frozen boots. The Pallid Hart sits where two roads cross in the mountains — the north road from the villages and the old pilgrim trail from the west. The locals say crossroads are cursed places. Where the devil walks at midnight, offering bargains to the desperate.

Nobody travels these forests at night. The wolves are bold, the paths treacherous, and the old-timers whisper of things far worse than wolves that move between the trees after dark. Yet here you all are — strangers driven together by a blizzard that fell like a hammer.

The common room is cramped, low-ceilinged, dark wood. A fire crackles in the stone hearth, casting long shadows that jump and stretch across the walls. Behind the bar, a thin, dark-haired woman grips a glass she has been polishing far too long. Her eyes dart to every sound. In the far corner, almost lost in shadow, a hooded figure sits motionless at a table. They have not moved since you arrived. They have not spoken.

You do not know each other's names. But you have been watching. You notice things.

checks.success: The innkeeper is terrified. Not of the storm — of something else. She keeps glancing at the cellar door and then quickly away, as if forcing herself not to look.
checks.fail: The innkeeper seems nervous, but it could just be the storm.

checks.success: The hooded stranger in the corner has not moved since you arrived. Not a muscle. You are not even sure he is breathing. His plate of food sits untouched.
checks.fail: Just a tired traveler in a hood. Nothing unusual.

checks.success: You notice the other travelers in the room. Each arrived alone. Each seems to carry their own weight of trouble. You can observe what they look like and how they carry themselves.
checks.fail: Just a room full of strangers sheltering from the storm.

playerDescriptions._note: When a player succeeds on Perception DC 10, describe the OTHER PCs using appearance data from their character sheets. Each player sees different descriptions based on who they are looking at. Use anonymous mode — no names, just physical observations.
playerDescriptions._equipment_note: Barry's gear (stakes, holy water, mirror, manacles) is visible if someone succeeds Perception DC 12 — describe as 'unusual equipment for a traveler' without naming it as monster-hunting gear.

beats.dmNotes: START HERE. All PCs are already in the tavern. Anonymous token mode is ON — they see 'Traveler' instead of names. Katya the minstrel is performing when the session begins — strumming her lute and singing old folk songs about the mountains, the crossroads, and the legends of the region. She provides ambient atmosphere and drops lore that attentive players can pick up on (the devil at crossroads, the strigoi, the white stag, the lost monastery). Read the opening, then go around the table asking each player: 'What does your character look like? What are they doing right now?' Use their answers + character sheet appearance data to describe them to the OTHER players. Then let them explore — talk to patrons, observe each other, ask Marta for another drink. The mystery begins with observation, not action.

beats.name: Faces in the Firelight
beats.readAloud: Besides yourselves, the common room holds a handful of other souls driven in by the storm. A stout merchant sits at a table near the wall, counting coins with shaking hands, muttering about lost shipments and dangerous roads. A gaunt man in a pilgrim's robe sits with his hands clasped over a worn wooden holy symbol, lips moving in silent prayer — he has not stopped praying since you arrived. A young woman with a lute leans against the hearth, watching everyone with bright, curious eyes and a half-smile that does not quite reach them. Near the fire, an old farmer hunches over an untouched bowl of stew, staring at nothing — his face grey, his eyes hollow. By the door, a broad-shouldered trapper in a fur-lined coat sits rigid, his jaw clenched, his eyes flicking constantly to the shuttered windows. And then there is the stranger. The hooded figure in the deepest shadow. He has not moved. He has not spoken. Marta seems to pretend he is not there.

npcDescriptions.henryk: A stout, balding man in a fur-lined merchant's coat. Mid-forties. Ruddy-faced from drink. His hands shake when he is not gripping his mug. A leather satchel of goods sits under his table — he checks it constantly. Rings on his fingers. Sweat on his brow despite the cold.
npcDescriptions.aldric: A gaunt, pale man in a simple pilgrim's robe, perhaps thirty. Tonsured hair. A wooden holy symbol of the Church hangs at his chest — he grips it like a drowning man grips driftwood. His eyes are closed in prayer but his knuckles are white. A small flask at his belt. His faith is a physical presence around him.
npcDescriptions.katya: A young woman, mid-twenties, with sharp dark eyes and travel-worn leather armor. A lute across her back. Her fingers move constantly — tapping, fidgeting, touching her belt where a rapier hangs. She watches everyone the way a hawk watches a field. When she catches your eye, she smiles. It is the smile of someone cataloguing you.
npcDescriptions.gregor: An old man. Seventies at least. Weathered skin like cracked leather. Farmer's hands — thick, scarred, strong despite his age. He smells of livestock and cheap ale. His bowl of stew sits untouched and cold. He stares into the fire with the expression of a man attending his own funeral.
npcDescriptions.tomas: A broad-shouldered man in his thirties, dressed in a trapper's heavy coat. A thick dark beard. Deep-set eyes that never rest — they move from the windows to the door to the cellar and back. Fresh scratches on his forearms, visible when his sleeves ride up. He drinks water, not ale. He sits nearest the door, as if ready to bolt. His hands clench and unclench in a slow rhythm.
npcDescriptions.vladislav: A hooded figure in the far corner, sitting in shadow so deep the firelight does not seem to reach it. A tall man — that much is clear from the length of his frame, even seated. A plate of food sits before him, untouched. A full mug. He does not eat. He does not drink. He does not move. Beneath the hood, nothing is visible except, occasionally, the faint glint of eyes catching the firelight. Watching.

checks.success: Henryk the merchant is putting on a brave face but his hands are shaking. Brother Aldric's prayers have an urgency to them — he is genuinely frightened. Katya is studying everyone, cataloguing details. Gregor looks like a man who has already given up.
checks.fail: The patrons seem tired but otherwise normal travelers sheltering from the storm.

checks.success: The trapper by the door — Tomas — keeps touching his forearms. He rolls his sleeves down the moment he notices you looking. His hands are clenched into fists.
checks.fail: Just another nervous traveler.

beats.dmNotes: This beat naturally follows the opening. After players describe themselves, describe the NPCs. Use the npcDescriptions above — read them as the players look around the room. Let players approach any patron. Katya is the most talkative and will approach PCs first. Gregor is doom-and-gloom. Tomas deflects everything. The stranger says nothing unless directly addressed. Marta offers stew and ale.

beats.name: Something Is Wrong
beats.readAloud: An hour passes. The fire burns lower. Marta adds no wood, though the pile beside the hearth is full. The ale is warm but the room is getting colder. The stranger still hasn't moved. Katya's song falters and stops — she says the acoustics in here are 'wrong.' Old Gregor pushes his untouched stew away and says, very quietly: 'Something happened here. Something bad. Can't you feel it?' No one answers him. But you can feel it. Something in the walls. Something waiting.

checks.success: A sound from below the floor — faint, rhythmic. Scratching. Like fingernails on stone. Then it stops.
checks.fail: You hear the wind and the fire. Nothing else.

checks.success: There's a cloth draped over the cellar door handle. Looking closer, you see scratches on the doorframe — deep ones, from the inside.
checks.fail: Nothing catches your eye.

checks.success: Marta flinched when the scratching sound came. She heard it too. And Tomas — Tomas went rigid, like a hunting dog catching a scent.
checks.fail: Hard to read anyone in this gloom.

beats.dmNotes: This beat should trigger naturally as players settle in. Mark it when players first express suspicion or unease. The scratching sound is key — it's Piotr in the cellar.

beats.name: The First Clue
beats.readAloud: You ask Marta about her husband. She freezes. The glass she was polishing slips and shatters on the floor. For a long moment, the only sound is the wind and the fire. Then, very quietly, with tears rolling down her cheeks: 'Piotr went down to the cellar three weeks ago. He never came back up. I can hear him down there, sometimes. Scratching. But that's not... that's not Piotr anymore. I know it isn't. I just... I don't know what to do.'

checks.success: She is telling the truth. Every word. The grief is real — but so is the terror. She has been living with this for three weeks, alone.
checks.fail: Sounds like she might be confused by grief.

checks.success: When Marta mentioned the cellar, the stranger's hand tightened on the edge of his table. Just for a moment. Then perfect stillness again.
checks.fail: The stranger didn't react.

checks.success: If something has been alive in the cellar for three weeks without food, it isn't alive in any normal sense of the word.
checks.fail: People can survive a long time without food. Probably.

beats.dmNotes: This can trigger from any conversation about Piotr, the cellar, or the missing travelers. Mark it when Marta reveals her husband is missing. Key clue — links to cellar_sounds and piotr_in_cellar secrets.

beats.name: The Investigation
beats.readAloud: The inn feels smaller now. The shadows deeper. The fire lower. Everyone in this room is hiding something — you can feel it. The cellar door is right there, the scratches on its frame clearly visible. The stranger watches. Marta wrings her hands. Tomas keeps checking the shuttered windows, as though expecting the moon. The question is no longer whether something is wrong. The question is: what are you going to do about it?

checks.success: Searching the stranger's corner of the room, you find that his plate of food is untouched — not a single bite. His mug is full. But most disturbing: the candle nearest him has gone out, and the wax is ice cold. As if the warmth is being drawn from it.
checks.fail: Nothing unusual about his table.

checks.success: Behind the bar, you find Marta's logbook. The last three entries show guests who checked in but never checked out. Their rooms are still made up, belongings still inside.
checks.fail: Just a normal innkeeper's ledger.

checks.success: You catch the stranger's reflection — or rather, you DON'T. In the brass serving plate behind the bar, everyone in the room is reflected. Everyone except him. Where he sits, there is only empty chair.
checks.fail: Nothing in the reflections catches your eye.

checks.success: Brother Aldric's holy symbol — he keeps touching it, unconsciously turning it toward the stranger. It's an instinct. Something in his faith is reacting to the presence in that corner.
checks.fail: Aldric seems devout but frightened, like everyone else.

beats.dmNotes: Let players drive this. They can search rooms, question NPCs, examine the cellar door, look for evidence. Each action should reveal a clue. The no-reflection clue is the big one — if anyone checks a mirror or reflective surface near Vladislav.

beats.name: The Escalation
beats.readAloud: The fire goes out. All at once, as if something exhaled on it. The room plunges into darkness. Someone screams — you think it's Katya. A crash of breaking glass. The shutters slam open and the blizzard howls in, driving snow across the floor. In the momentary flash of moonlight through the window, you see two things: the stranger's chair is empty — he was there a second ago — and Tomas is on his knees, clutching his head, a sound like a growl tearing from his throat. The shutters slam closed. Darkness again. And from the cellar, the scratching becomes pounding.

checks.success: In the darkness, you hear movement — inhumanly fast — near the ceiling. Something is above you.
checks.fail: The darkness is total. You can't see or hear anything over the wind.

checks.success: You keep your nerve. You can act.
checks.fail: Terror grips you. You are frozen for a moment, unable to act (lose your first round of actions).

checks.success: Feeling along the wall, you find a lantern. One match. One shot at light.
checks.fail: You can't find anything useful in the dark.

beats.dmNotes: This is the horror peak before combat. The fire going out should be dramatic — all lights in the room (Hubitat) go dark simultaneously. Let them fumble in darkness for 30-60 seconds of real time. Tomas is fighting his transformation. Vladislav has moved.

beats.name: The Cellar
beats.readAloud: The cellar stairs descend into a cold that has nothing to do with winter. The walls are ancient — older than the inn above, rough-hewn monastery stone stained dark with something you don't want to identify. At the bottom: a narrow passage, the ceiling so low you have to duck. The air smells of copper and rot. Chains hang from the walls — old, rusted. The scratching sound is louder now. Much louder. And then you see it: in the far corner, a makeshift coffin made of rough planks. Beside it, crouched in the shadows, is what was once a man. Piotr Kowalski stares at you with dead white eyes, his mouth open, two elongated canines gleaming in your torchlight. He hisses.

checks.success: The coffin contains a velvet lining, a sealed letter addressed to 'Houska,' and dried blood. This is not Piotr's coffin — it belongs to the stranger upstairs.
checks.fail: A coffin. Terrifying but you can't bring yourself to search it.

checks.success: A vampire spawn. Created by a vampire's bite. Piotr is no longer human — but a stronger vampire controls him. Destroy the master, and the spawn may find peace. Or they may simply go feral.
checks.fail: Undead. Very dangerous. That's all you know.

checks.success: Letters scattered on the floor, written in an old Slavic dialect. They reference 'the gathering at Houska' and 'the three seats of power.' There is a larger conspiracy here.
checks.fail: Nothing else in the cellar catches your eye.

beats.dmNotes: Piotr spawn attacks if approached within 10 feet. He is CR3 — dangerous but beatable. The letter to Houska is a campaign hook. If players search thoroughly, they find evidence of the three-castle conspiracy.

beats.name: The Revelation
beats.readAloud: Now you understand. The hooded stranger. The missing travelers. Piotr in the cellar. The dead, bloodless deer on the road. It was never the storm you needed to fear. It was never the cold. It was the thing sitting in the corner of this inn, watching you with ancient, hungry eyes, waiting for the right moment to feed. And now it knows that you know.

checks.success: Vampires are vulnerable to sunlight, running water, and stakes through the heart. They cannot enter a residence without invitation — but Vladislav was invited by Marta. Garlic repels them. Holy symbols wielded with faith cause them pain.
checks.fail: You know vampires are dangerous. That's about it.

checks.success: Brother Aldric's holy symbol could be a weapon if wielded with true faith. A prayer of turning could force the vampire back. But it requires genuine conviction — fear alone isn't enough.
checks.fail: You remember something about holy symbols and vampires but the details escape you.

beats.dmNotes: This beat fires when the vampire secret is definitively revealed — either through cellar evidence, caught feeding, no-reflection discovery, or direct confrontation. The revelation flash player effect should fire here.

beats.name: The Confrontation
beats.readAloud: Vladislav rises from his chair — or rather, he is suddenly standing, with no visible transition between sitting and standing. The hood falls back. His face is pale as bone, angular, beautiful in the way a knife is beautiful. His eyes are dark and old — centuries old. He smiles, and you see the fangs. 'I had hoped,' he says, his voice like silk over broken glass, 'to leave without unpleasantness. But you have made that... difficult.' The shadows in the room seem to lean toward him, as if drawn by gravity. The temperature drops. The fire, if it was still burning, dies.

checks.success: You stand your ground. Vladislav's smile falters — just for a moment. He is used to terror. Defiance surprises him.
checks.fail: His presence is overwhelming. Ancient. Predatory. Every instinct screams at you to run.

checks.success: You appeal to whatever humanity remains. For a moment — just a moment — something flickers in those dead eyes. Then it's gone. 'You remind me of someone I knew. Before.' He pauses. 'They died badly.'
checks.fail: He is beyond persuasion. He is beyond reason. He is hunger wearing a human face.

beats.dmNotes: Vladislav is CR13. He will NOT fight to the death — if reduced below 50 HP, he flees (mist form to cellar or out window). He fights smart: Charm on the strongest fighter, bite on the isolated, legendary actions to scatter the party. Call Piotr spawn if cellar wasn't cleared.

beats.name: The Battle for Survival
beats.readAloud: Combat erupts in the cramped common room. Tables shatter. The fire scatters embers across the floor. Vladislav moves like smoke — appearing, striking, vanishing. If Tomas hasn't already transformed, the violence and the moonlight push him over the edge. The patrons scream and scatter. Marta dives behind the bar. Brother Aldric raises his holy symbol with shaking hands. This is not a fight you were prepared for. This is survival. Hold until dawn — or die in the dark.

checks.success: You flip a heavy table to create cover, giving half-cover (+2 AC) to anyone behind it.
checks.fail: The table is heavier than you expected. No cover.

checks.success: You spot Vladislav's movement pattern — he always returns to the cellar door side. That's his escape route.
checks.fail: He's too fast to track.

checks.success: Brother Aldric's faith holds. His holy symbol blazes with light — Vladislav recoils (disadvantage on attacks against Aldric this round).
checks.fail: Aldric's faith wavers. The symbol is just metal.

beats.dmNotes: This is the final combat. Vladislav (CR13), potentially Piotr spawn (CR3), potentially transformed Tomas (CR3 werewolf). Use the combat AI system for NPC tactics. If Vladislav escapes, mark hook-vladislav-escape as foreshadowed. If Tomas transforms, play for tragedy — he didn't want this.

beats.name: Dawn Breaks
beats.readAloud: The first light creeps through the shattered shutters. Pale. Cold. But light. The blizzard is dying — the wind a whisper where it was a scream. If Vladislav still stands, he recoils from the dawn, hissing, retreating to the cellar or crumbling to ash. The horror is over. But the cost is written on every face in this room — the blood on the floor, the broken furniture, the empty chairs where people sat just hours ago. Marta is sobbing. Tomas, if he survived, is human again, naked and shaking in the corner, remembering what he did. The mountain road is visible through the broken door. The snow has stopped. The sun is rising. You are alive. And you will never forget this night.
beats.dmNotes: Let the players breathe. This is the denouement. Award XP. Resolve NPC fates — who lived, who died, who was transformed. Marta's reaction depends on Piotr's fate. If the Houska letter was found, plant the seed for the next arc. The party is forged — strangers who survived together.

atmosphere.startNote: Session begins in tavern_warm — fire crackling, Katya performing, ale flowing. Shift to tavern_tense as clues emerge. The warm start makes the descent into horror more effective.
